page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c8ae.16xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance built on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9T34 processor. The instance provides 64 physical cores with one thread per core, resulting in 64 dedicated vCPUs operating at a base clock speed of 3.4 GHz.

This instance is configured with 128 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, requiring external block storage for operating systems and data. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 32 Gbps, and the instance does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

The dedicated CPU allocation and 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio position this instance as a cost-efficient option for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. The hardware profile is suited for high-performance computing, batch processing, intensive web serving, and distributed analytics where dedicated physical cores prevent resource contention.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c8ae.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 64 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9T34 processor running at 3.4 GHz. Utilizing the KVM hypervisor on an x86_64 architecture, this instance provides 64 physical cores with a single thread per core, ensuring dedicated CPU allocation without hyperthreading. It is equipped with 128 GB of RAM, establishing a 2.0 GB per core ratio. The instance does not include local storage or GPUs, relying instead on network-attached storage, and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 32 Gbps. This hardware configuration is designed for compute-intensive workloads such as batch processing, high-performance computing, and web serving, offering a balanced resource density for tasks requiring dedicated processing power.

tagline: A dedicated 64-core AMD EPYC compute instance with a 1:2 memory-to-CPU ratio and 32 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.

128 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core
32 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput connectivity
No local storage or integrated GPUs, requiring external block storage

Suited for high-performance computing, batch processing, and web serving
